title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
During an interrogation, Nancy Duane refuses to discuss the deaths of a priest and his wife, as well as Jennie Hobbs from Gleechol. She asks Poirot about the deaths of Harry, Edith, and Richard, who Poirot confirms were poisoned. Nancy mentions her late father, Algernon Johnson, and discusses an amateur painting of him that she keeps because he is smiling in it.

Police officer Stanley Bill interrupts to inform Poirot that two keys, engraved with room numbers 121 and 317 from the Bluxton Hotel, were found in the pocket of a blue fur-collared coat belonging to Mrs. Dunn. Poirot reveals that two murders occurred in those specific rooms. He notes that a witness saw Nancy dropping keys numbered 101 and 301 near the Blaxland Hotel on the night of the crime. Nancy claims she is being framed by someone who dislikes Harriet, Ada, and Richard. Poirot decides not to arrest her immediately, noting it would be illogical for a killer to keep such incriminating keys.

Poirot travels to the Wallace household to speak with Louisa Wallace. He observes the chaotic state of the house and the clumsiness of their new maid, Dorcas. Louisa shows Poirot a portrait painted by Nancy Duane. Regarding the night of the crime, Louisa states that Nancy arrived at six o'clock to deliver the painting and stayed until approximately ten o'clock. Louisa asserts that at 8:30, she, Nancy, and Saint John were all sitting together at the table, providing an alibi for Nancy.
